zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Bertram於大約1225年出生在英國諾圖波藍德米特福德時，他的父親Roger Bertram I39歲，母親Agnes de Emmely17歲。他於大約1243年與Ida de Ros結婚。他去世於1273年在英國諾薩普頓史爾享年49歲，埋葬在英國諾圖波藍德米特福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger Bertram于大约1225年出生在英国諾圖波藍德米特福德时，他的父亲Roger Bertram I39岁，母亲Agnes de Emmely17岁。他于大约1243年与Ida de Ros结婚。他去世于1273年在英国諾薩普頓史爾享年49岁，埋葬在英国諾圖波藍德米特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Bertram was born about 1225, in Mitford, Northumberland, England, his father, Roger Bertram I, was 39 and his mother, Agnes de Emmely, was 17. He married Ida de Ros about 1243. He died in 1273, in Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 49, and was buried in Mitford, Northumberland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Bertram est né environ 1225, à Mitford, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Roger Bertram I, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Agnes d’Emmely, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Ida de Ros environ 1243. Il est décédé en 1273, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Mitford, Northumberland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger Bertram um 1225 in Mitford, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger Bertram I 39 und seine Mutter Agnes de Emmely 17. Er hat um 1243 Ida de Ros geheiratet. Er starb 1273 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Northamptonshire, England und wurde in Mitford, Northumberland, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Bertram è nato il circa 1225 nel Mitford, Northumberland, England, il suo padre, Roger Bertram I, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de Emmely, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Ida de Ros circa 1243. Morì nel 1273, nel Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Mitford, Northumberland, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Bertramが約1225年にイギリスノースウンバーランドミトフォードで生まれた時，父Roger Bertram Iは39歳，母Agnes de Emmelyは17歳であった。約1243年にIda de Rosと結婚した。彼は1273年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで49歳で死去し，イギリスノースウンバーランドミトフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Bertram(이)가 대략 1225년 영국 노르스벌랜드 미트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Bertram I(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Emmely(은)는 17세였다. 그는 대략 1243년 Ida de Ros(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1273년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르스벌랜드 미트퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Bertram nasceu aproximadamente 1225, em Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Bertram I, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Emmely, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Ida de Ros aproximadamente 1243. Ele faleceu em 1273, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Bertram родился около 1225 г. в местности митфорд, нортумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger Bertram I было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Emmely, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Ida de Ros около 1243 г. Он умер в 1273 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности митфорд, нортумбирланд, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger Bertram nació aproximadamente 1225, en Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Bertram I, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Agnes de Emmely, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Ida de Ros aproximadamente 1243. Murió en 1273, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultado en Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra.
